heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 106–224, title IV, § 438(a)(7)114 Stat. 454June 20, 2000
label: 1651 to 1656 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 106–224, title IV, § 438(a)(7)114 Stat. 454June 20, 2000
notes: Section 1651, , , set forth Federal Government policy to control, suppress, and eradicate the poisonous weed Halogeton glomeratus.act July 14, 1952, ch. 721, § 266 Stat. 597

Section 1652, , , set forth provisions relating to authority of Secretaries of Agriculture and the Interior and requiring consent prior to conducting measures and operations to control, suppress, or eradicate the weed.act July 14, 1952, ch. 721, § 366 Stat. 598

Section 1653, , , authorized discretionary expenditure of funds and acceptance of voluntary and uncompensated services of Federal, State, and local employees.act July 14, 1952, ch. 721, § 466 Stat. 598
Section 1654, , , gave Secretaries discretion to require contributions by States as prerequisites to making Federal expenditures.act July 14, 1952, ch. 721, § 566 Stat. 598
Section 1655, , , authorized appropriations and set forth provisions relating to their use.act July 14, 1952, ch. 721, § 666 Stat. 598
Section 1656, , , set forth provisions relating to extent of authority of this chapter.act July 14, 1952, ch. 721, § 766 Stat. 599

Short Title, , which provided that this chapter could be cited as the “Halogeton Glomeratus Control Act”, was repealed by , , .Act July 14, 1952, ch. 721, § 166 Stat. 597Pub. L. 106–224, title IV, § 438(a)(7)114 Stat. 454June 20, 2000
